name: Ceramide capsules
oneliner: Modest hydration data. Topical ceramides outperform per rupee.
headline: Ceramide capsules
dek: Wheat- or rice-derived ceramide capsules with small but reproducible TEWL-reduction endpoints. The mechanism is plausible, the trial data is honest, and the cost-effectiveness comparison against a CeraVe tub is unforgiving. A defensible adjunct; never a primary.
bottom: A defensible adjunct, never a foundation. Spend the money on a topical ceramide cream first; consider this only if topical optimisation has plateaued.

evidence.note: Modest, replicated improvement in stratum corneum hydration. The trial data is honest.
Small but consistent shift. Topical ceramide moisturisers produce larger endpoints at a fraction of the cost.
Promising signal; trials are too small for confident recommendation. Reasonable trial in mild atopy.
Not the indication. Marketing extrapolation that the literature does not support.

forms.note: Gluten-free alternative. Equivalent endpoints in head-to-head trials.
Higher-dose marketing fork. Diminishing returns above 30 mg/d.

faq.q: Why not just use a topical?
faq.a: You almost certainly should. A tub of CeraVe Moisturising Cream applied twice daily delivers the ceramides where they actually matter — at the stratum corneum — at a fraction of the supplement's price. Oral ceramides may have a marginal additive role in stubborn eczema or generalised dryness, but the topical foundation should always come first.
12 weeks at 30 mg/d. Hydration shifts can be measured by week 6; the visible textural endpoints lag behind. Photograph at week 0 and week 12 for honest comparison.

There is no safety advantage — both are safe. The supplement category does avoid the formulation politics (ingredient irritation, fragrance) that some sensitive-skin patients face with topicals, which is a real if narrow use-case.

faq.q: Can I combine with topical ceramides?
faq.a: Yes, with no interaction concern. The two work via different mechanisms: oral ceramides increase the substrate pool available for skin synthesis; topical ceramides directly fill the stratum corneum. Stack if you want; the marginal benefit is modest.
